paper_title: Boundary rigidity of 3D CAT(0) cube complexes
summary: The CAT(0) case of Question 1 has been fully resolved: Chalopin and Chepoi (arXiv:2310.04223) proved that all finite CAT(0) cube complexes of any dimension are reconstructible up to isomorphism from their boundary distances (computed in the 1-skeleton), explicitly confirming the conjecture of Haslegrave–Scott–Tamitegama–Tan and in particular settling the open k≥4 cases. The broader question of what conditions beyond CAT(0) might suffice, and whether CAT(0) is in any sense necessary, remains open.

since_posted.title: Boundary rigidity of finite CAT(0) cube complexes
since_posted.claim: Proves that all finite CAT(0) cube complexes of any dimension k can be reconstructed from their boundary distances via median-graph corner-peeling techniques, resolving the k≥4 open case of Question 1 under the CAT(0) condition.

search_queries: CAT(0) cube complex boundary rigidity boundary distances reconstructible 2309.04385
boundary rigidity cube complex higher dimensional k-dimensional reconstructible combinatorial type arxiv 2024 2025
notes: Chalopin–Chepoi (arXiv:2310.04223) resolve the CAT(0) case for all k, explicitly citing this as a conjecture of Haslegrave–Scott–Tamitegama–Tan. The paper also appears to have a journal version listed on ScienceDirect (pii S0095895624000625, likely JCTB 2024), though this was not verified by WebFetch. The original Question 1 is broader—asking for a full characterization of conditions—so the status is 'partial'; a complete necessary-and-sufficient characterization remains open.
